excerpt: every organ of the human body is arranged in the most purposeful manner for its function
source_work: Rahat al-ʿAql (The Comfort of the Intellect)
source_location_hint: Discussions of human physiology and divine wisdom in creation

excerpt: the body becomes altered, producing foul air and odor, and is then placed in the earth where it dissolves into its four elements
source_location_hint: Discussions of death, the body, and elemental dissolution

excerpt: This world is a womb for the Hereafter (al-dunyā raḥm al-ākhira)
source_location_hint: Sayings concerning the relationship between worldly and otherworldly existence

excerpt: Death is the final threshold of the physical world; the soul enters the Hereafter only by separating from the body
source_work: Asas al-Taʾwil (The Foundation of Esoteric Exegesis)
source_location_hint: On the metaphysical necessity of death and transition to the Hereafter

excerpt: The higher partial soul perfects the lower partial soul, causing it to transform from matter into form
source_location_hint: On the emanation and perfection of souls in hierarchical order

excerpt: The soul that was only soul becomes form when it reaches its limit and develops as a form in the Hereafter
source_work: Kitab al-ʿAlim wa-l-Ghulam (The Book of Master and Disciple)
source_location_hint: Dialogue on spiritual perfection and transformation

excerpt: People are asleep, and when they die, they awake (al-nās niyām fa-idhā māt inṣabbat)
source_location_hint: Prophetic sayings and their interpretation in the Majalis

excerpt: You were created for eternity, yet you travel from house to house: from loins to wombs, from wombs to the world, from the world to the Barzakh, and from the Barzakh to Paradise or Hell
source_author: al-Sharif al-Radi (compiler), attributed to ʿAlī ibn Abī Ṭālib
source_location_hint: Sermon on the stages of creation and the eternal journey of the soul

excerpt: All the knowledge of the Messenger of Allah is buried and concealed within Ali
source_location_hint: discussion of spiritual knowledge transmission through the Imam

excerpt: The soul purifies the waste of the body through ablution and care, protecting it from countless calamities
source_location_hint: exposition on the soul's stewardship of the body

excerpt: The body is ignorant and does not recognize the soul's greatness, being like a disbeliever who cannot acknowledge the divine
source_location_hint: chapter on the soul and its relation to the body

excerpt: Death is a blessing and mercy for virtuous souls who have laboured to perfect the body during life
source_location_hint: meditation on death and the soul's liberation

excerpt: The soul bears diseases and wounds, and the ordinances of religion are remedies for curing the ailments of the self
source_location_hint: discussion of spiritual pathology and theological cure
